title: rhytidophyllum earlei
intro: Rhytidophyllum earlei, also known as Earle's leafy liverwort, is a species of liverwort that belongs to the family Dendrocerotaceae. It is a type of non-vascular, thallose liverwort that is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The medicinal properties of Rhytidophyllum earlei include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as improving respiratory issues and reducing fever. Rhytidophyllum earlei is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and its herbal preparations are also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
